actors.name: Frontend Developer
actors.description: The user interacting with the Component Atlas UI.

actors.name: Generation Engine
actors.description: The backend system orchestrating the AI LLM tasks.

preconditions: The Frontend Developer is authenticated via AWS Identity Center.
The developer has navigated to a specific Component Inspector View in the UI.
The underlying Figma node ID exists in the active_component_atlas database.

mainFlow.action: Disables the button, displays a loading spinner, and sends a POST request to the Generation Engine API.
Fetches the latest normalized graph data for the component from the database.
Spins up an ephemeral agent container and passes the graph data to the LLM.
Receives the generated React AST, validates the syntax, and saves the new version to the database.
Returns an HTTP 200 Success response with the new code string.
Updates the CodeSnippetBlock with the new code, removes the spinner, and displays a success toast notification.

alternateFlows.name: No Upstream Design Changes
alternateFlows.condition: The Generation Engine detects that the Figma metadata has not changed since the last generation timestamp.

steps.action: Skips the LLM request to save API costs and immediately retrieves the existing code from the cache.
Returns HTTP 200 Success with a flag indicating 'Cached Response'.

exceptions.name: LLM Rate Limit Exceeded
exceptions.condition: The external LLM provider returns a 429 Too Many Requests error.

handlingSteps.action: Logs the rate limit event and aborts the agent execution.
Returns an HTTP 429 error response to the UI.
Re-enables the 'Regenerate Code' button and displays a red toast notification: 'AI Provider is currently busy. Please try again in 1 minute.'

exceptions.name: AST Validation Failure
exceptions.condition: The LLM returns code that fails the internal Babel/TypeScript compilation check.

handlingSteps.action: Flags the generated code as INVALID in the database and prevents it from overwriting the current good state.
Returns an HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity response containing the compilation error details.
Displays the error in the GenerationLogsPanel and prompts the user to either retry or submit a manual bug report.

onSuccess: The system database contains a new version record for the generated component.
The user's UI accurately reflects the newly generated code.
onFailure: The component's previous stable code state remains untouched.
The user is clearly informed of why the generation failed.
